title: Add Page Size and Orientation Preferences
body:
## Overview
This PR adds comprehensive page size and orientation preferences to the Print2Paper4VSCode extension, fixing the jsPDF dimension error and providing users with flexible printing options.

## Key Features

### 🎯 Page Size Preferences
- **5 Most Popular Sizes**: A4, Letter, Legal, A3, A5
- **Locale-Based Defaults**: US/CA/MX → Letter, others → A4
- **Proper Units**: Inches for US sizes, mm for metric sizes
- **Persistent Storage**: User preferences saved across sessions

### 🎨 Orientation Preferences
- **Portrait/Landscape**: Custom SVG icons for visual clarity
- **Dynamic Switching**: Real-time PDF regeneration
- **YAML Storage**: Icons stored in maintainable YAML files

### 🔧 Technical Improvements
- **Fixed jsPDF Dimension Error**: Dynamic page height calculation instead of fixed 10000mm
- **Content Truncation**: Handles content exceeding jsPDF 14400 user unit limit
- **ES6 Shorthand**: Cleaner code with modern JavaScript syntax
- **Comprehensive Testing**: 137 tests passing (100% success rate)

## Files Changed
- `src/PDF.ts` - Page dimensions, unit selection, height calculation
- `src/PaperPrinter.ts` - Preferences, menu items, selection handlers  
- `src/VSCodeAPIs.ts` - Added `getLocale()` method
- `src/PaperPrinter.yaml` - SVG icons for orientation
- `tests/` - 2 new comprehensive test files

## Testing
- ✅ All 137 tests passing
- ✅ Comprehensive test coverage for new functionality
- ✅ Integration tests for end-to-end workflow
- ✅ Error handling and edge case testing

## Breaking Changes
None - this is a purely additive feature that maintains backward compatibility.

## Screenshots
The new Page and Orient menus will appear in the PDF preview toolbar, allowing users to:
- Select from 5 popular page sizes with proper dimensions displayed
- Switch between Portrait and Landscape orientations with visual icons
- Have their preferences automatically saved and restored

## Related Issues
Fixes the jsPDF dimension error that occurred with large content by implementing proper page height calculation and content truncation.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

- **New Features**
  - Page & Orientation menus with icons, persisted preferences, orientation-aware PDF rendering, and in-place PDF content updates via the viewer.

- **Improvements**
  - Locale-aware page-size defaults and unit handling (mm/in), smarter pagination with truncation notices, normalized PDF colors, modular toolbar assets (CSS/JS) with drag and flyout interactions, and refined tiered timing display.

- **Tests**
  - Expanded integration and unit tests covering PDF page sizes/orientations, menus, panel management, and locale handling.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

title: Fix PDF worker URI conversion and refactor App component management
body:
## Summary

This PR fixes the PDF worker loading issue and refactors the App component management system for better maintainability and type safety.

## Key Changes

### Bug Fixes
- **Fixed PDF worker URI conversion**: Resolved issue where as_uri patterns weren't being converted to webview URIs
- **Improved regex precision**: Added word boundaries to prevent matching workerSrc in JavaScript code
- **Preserved template patterns**: Ensured as_uri patterns are properly processed by the URI conversion system

### Refactoring
- **App component management**: Replaced individual component calls with array-based initialization and cleanup
- **Type safety improvements**: Replaced any type with proper components_t type definition
- **Single source of truth**: One componentOrder array for both init and done operations
- **Cleaner code**: Reduced duplication and improved maintainability

### Dependencies
- **Added local PDF worker**: src/lib/pdf.worker.min.js for offline operation
- **Version matching**: Ensured PDF.js library and worker versions match (3.11.174)

## Technical Details

The issue was that the src attribute regex was incorrectly matching workerSrc assignments in JavaScript code, converting the template pattern before the as_uri replacement step could process it.

Solution: Added word boundaries to only match actual HTML src attributes, not JavaScript assignments.

## Testing
- All 96 tests pass
- PDF worker loads correctly in webview
- Type safety maintained throughout
- No breaking changes to existing functionality

## Files Changed
- src/OS.ts - Fixed regex and URI conversion logic
- src/App.ts - Refactored component management with proper typing
- src/lib/pdf.worker.min.js - Added local PDF worker file
- Various YAML templates - Updated for new URI conversion system

## Impact
- PDF viewer now works correctly with local worker
- Improved code maintainability and type safety
- Better separation of concerns in component initialization
- Reduced code duplication

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

- **New Features**
  - In-memory PDF generation with PDF.js preview and a draggable toolbar for interactive print preview.
  - Export-to-PDF path from editor content with real-time theme/font updates and unified theming.

- **Improvements**
  - Cross-platform clipboard and OS integration for more reliable capture and printing.
  - URI-safe webview resource handling for consistent asset loading.

- **Documentation**
  - Major README and architecture docs overhaul with execution flow, components, dependencies, and debugging notes.

- **Chores**
  - Dev dependency and typings updates.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

title: Assess pdf generation migration
body: Migrate PDF generation to jsPDF for vector output, removing external browser dependencies and improving print preview fidelity. The previous Chromium/Puppeteer-based solution introduced complexity with external browser management, platform-specific dependencies, and inconsistent rendering. This PR refactors the PDF generation to use jsPDF directly from Shiki tokens, ensuring vector-based, in-memory PDF creation that is perfectly synchronized between the webview preview and the final printed output. This also cleans up dependencies and improves type safety. --- ## Summary by CodeRabbit - **New Features** - Linux support added; PDF generation now uses jsPDF (Node.js) with in-memory PDFs. - Save dialog to choose PDF output; enhanced syntax highlighting via Shiki. - **Changes** - Print/preview flows are PDF-first; previews no longer printed from rendered HTML. - Clipboard capture now plain-text only (RTF removed). - **Documentation** - README/docs updated to reflect jsPDF/Node.js prerequisites and workflow. - **Chores** - Removed RTF-related deps; added jspdf and Shiki typings. - **Tests** - Extensive new test suites covering OS platforms, PDF pipeline, Stylize, UI, and integrations.

title: Refactor codebase to use Diagnostic class
body: Add a comprehensive refactor plan to upgrade the codebase to use the new `Diagnostics` class. --- ## Summary by CodeRabbit - Documentation - Added a Diagnostics Refactor Plan describing a phased migration to a standardized diagnostics system. - Chores - Replaced internal UI logging with a centralized diagnostics system across the app and tests; startup/cleanup logs now use diagnostics. - Removed the built-in history/persistence feature (history entries and automatic history storage were removed), so recent-file history/persistence is no longer available.

title: Fix Diagnostics class: lineage display, debug state inheritance, and error message format
body:
## Summary

This PR fixes several critical issues in the Diagnostics class:

### Issues Fixed
- **Lineage Display**: Now properly shows `ParentClass > childMethod > grandchildMethod` instead of duplicated names
- **Debug State Inheritance**: Fixed dynamic inheritance from parent instances so debug state can change at runtime
- **Error Message Format**: Corrected missing key messages to show `❌ missing: "key", "key", "key"` format
- **Constructor Order**: Fixed initialization order to prevent circular dependencies

### Technical Details
- Moved `debugOn()` call to after lineage building to avoid circular dependency
- Updated `crawlUp()` method to properly access parent names and debug states through public methods
- Changed `require()` method to use `this.print()` instead of `this.out()` for validation messages
- Fixed test expectations to match the correct output format

### Testing
- All 72 tests passing
- Diagnostics class now works correctly for all use cases
- No more circular dependency issues during construction

### Impact
- Improves debugging and logging throughout the extension
- Fixes issues that could cause silent failures in diagnostic output
- Maintains backward compatibility while fixing core functionality

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Added hierarchical Diagnostics (runtime logging/validation), async dropdown toolbar with active-item indicators, theme-aware printing, and an environment tooling config enabling ESLint/Prettier and snapshot updates.

* **Refactor**
  * Centralized webview messaging and async menu rendering, consolidated UI types, migrated to official VS Code typings, removed legacy stub-generation and switched many handlers to string-return flows.

* **Style**
  * Updated menu/button visuals, toolbar drag behavior, menu layouts, and icon/text presentation.

* **Documentation**
  * Added Diagnostics usage guide and a phased named-parameter refactor plan.

* **Tests**
  * New Diagnostics tests and updated UI/menu tests to match async APIs.

* **Chores**
  * Removed related scripts/command from package configuration.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

title: Implement diagnostics logging utility
body: Add `Diagnostics` class to provide structured logging with class, method, and timestamp context. --- ## Summary by CodeRabbit - New Features - Introduces structured, hierarchical debug logging with global and per-instance toggles. - Supports nested method contexts, timestamped and formatted messages, and optional execution timing. - Allows inheritance/override of debug state, fluent chaining, and an option to always log regardless of debug state. - Tests - Adds comprehensive tests covering state management, context inheritance, timing accuracy, output behavior, async isolation, and chaining.

title: Upgrade shiki to latest edm version
body: Upgrade the extension to ES Modules (EDM) and Shiki v3, implementing on-demand language loading and generic regex-based theme filtering for enhanced performance, maintainability, and dynamic theme/language support. This refactor moves the extension to modern JavaScript modules and the latest Shiki API, addressing previous limitations such as hardcoded language and theme lists. It introduces lazy loading for Shiki languages, direct consumption of VSCode theme objects, and a flexible regex-based filtering mechanism for themes, while also standardizing internal terminology to "styler" for clarity. --- ## Summary by CodeRabbit - **New Features** - Draggable toolbar with standardized menus and a dynamic theme picker; centralized UI templating. - Upgraded syntax highlighting (Shiki v3) with on-demand language loading and unified Shiki+VSCode theme discovery. - Clipboard capture that converts selection/RTF/plain text into printable HTML. - New command: Generate VS Code API Stubs. - **Changes** - Print/preview flow moved to a UI-driven, message-based experience; command IDs/keybindings updated. - **Documentation** - Installation guide added. - **Tests** - Extensive unit, integration, and validation suites added. - **Chores** - ESM/TypeScript/tooling updates, dependency upgrades, build/test script changes, publisher set.
